As a Ronaldo fan, let us unite Messi fans, because the same reasons we admire Ronaldo seriously overlap as to why you admire Messi. A true Ronaldo fan and lover of the sport should admire Messi as a footballer and vice versa.

Ronaldo, at age 15, had to have heart surgery for a heart condition. Grew up in poverty, had an alcoholic father who he lost when he was young and the memory enough to still make him cry.

Messi was bullied, had a growth hormone deficiency, and look at how strong he has become.

Football is like Tennis, it got lucky with MULTIPLE GOATS, playing the same time, with different attributes , but no weaknesses.

text: A verdade dói para quem é fã de marketing, mas os fatos são soberanos: Messi é um produto do Barcelona, e muito mal acabado por sinal. Precisou de redoma de vidro, hormônios e dos melhores 'garçons' da história (Xavi e Iniesta) para brilhar. Se o Cristiano Ronaldo tivesse esses passes perfeitos, teria 10.000 gols e 20 Bolas de Ouro, pois é o talento completo. Já o Messi, se tivesse que encarar os 'camelôs' do United ou o caos de outros times como o CR7 encarou, não passaria de um Romero: um jogador correria, mal acabado, que joga de cabeça baixa por não ter a estatura mental e física de um Rei. Cristiano é o maior da história de todos os esportes; o resto é produto de laboratório que só funciona em estufa.

text:
Let's end the Goat Debate,

Messi has 800 goals
Ronaldo has 900 goals, but! Messi played 200 less games than Ronaldo. When Messi plays that much games like Ronaldo, see the goals Messi scored

In assists, there is obviously no debate
Messi clears

Let's talk about the attributes (like shoot, pass etc)
Shooting - Ronaldo (1)
Passing - Messi (1)
Dribbling - Messi (2)
finishing - Ronaldo (2)
Playmaking - Messi (3)
Pace - Ronaldo (3)
Free Kicks - Messi (4)
Penalty - Ronaldo (4)
Vision - Messi (5)
Better - Messi

Let's talk about who played better in all years:-
2008 - Ronaldo (1)
2009,10,11,12 - Messi  (4)
2013,14 - Ronaldo (3)
2015 - Messi (5)
2016,17,18 - Ronaldo (6)
2019,20 - Messi (7)
2021 - Ronaldo (7)
2022 - Messi (8)
Winner - Messi (2023,24,25 Messi and Ronaldo Were Not Playing In Europe)

Let's talk about Trophies And Awards!
Ballon Do'rs - Messi 8>5 Ronaldo
Ovr Trophies - Messi 46>33 Ronaldo
Golden Boots - Messi 6>4 Ronaldo
UCL - Ronaldo 5>4 Messi
League Titles - Messi 12>7 Ronaldo
Int. Trophies - Messi 4>2 Ronaldo

Let's prove that Messi didn't rob any Ballon Do'rs!
2010 - 
Iniesta was injured for a long time and missed world cup & club world cup matches, hence Messi was more important and more impactful for Barcelona
Messi: UCL Top scorer (12 goals) total stats: Most G/A (60 goals, 17 assists) League Stats: Top Scorer (40+ goals)

According to haters Messi robbed because of that one iniesta goal in WC final. Overall Messi was better at that season

2015 -
Messi stats - 58 Goals, 31 Assists
Suarez stats - 25 Goals, *24 Assists
Neymar stats - 39 goals, 11 assists

Haters say neymar deserved but when you actually check the stats, even Suarez has more g/a then neymar so how neymar deserved!!! 

2019 -
Barca was so bad that Messi had to carry, if dembele was good then they would've won the UCL too (cuz spurs is not So good)
• Most G/a In that season
• Laliga Top scorer
• UCL top scorer
• Almost Won the Treble (Laliga & Spanish Super Cup)

2021 -
Goals - Lewandowski
Assists - Messi
Chances Created - Messi
Big Chances Created - Messi
dribbles - Messi
MOTM - Messi
Individual - Messi

In terms of club, It was a close battle but What Did lewandowski got in international?! Messi won Copa America

2023 -
G/A During Ballon Do'r Voting Period:
Messi - 68 , Haaland - 67

Haaland semi final and Final Stats:
(UCL)
0 goals vs rma leg 1&2
0 goals vs inter milan
(FA Cup)
0 goals vs Sheffield United
0 goals vs Man United
(SUPER CUP)
0 goals vs Sevilla
< also Zero assists >

Messi world Cup stats:
Group stage - MOTM, R16 - MOTM, QF - MOTM, SF - MOTM, FINAL - MOTM

+ world cup is one of the most important thing for the Ballon Do'r
Isn't this enough!!!

Haters : "give me penalty" (In WC)
My ans: 
Messi in Qatar 2022:
7 goals (4 penalties) WC Champion, WC Golden Ball

Ronaldo in euro 2020:
5 goals (3 penalties) Last 16 exit, Golden Boot winner 

According to Ronaldo fans, was Cristiano 1 penalty away from winning/rigging euro 2020 or what?!

Let's start some fun facts!
1) odegaard once said: "they don't want mbappe to win WC, they don't want haaland win bdor, they just want Messi to lose🥶
2) Ronaldo fans have some excuses like "puskas", "UCL all time top scorer" and "5 ucl" 
But I have answers too! If puskas existed in 2007 Messi would've won (Ankara Messi) if 2019 Barca was good Messi would've won 5th UCL. Obviously a striker would have more goals than a winger!!
3) Messi played 200 less games than Ronaldo still have only 100 goals less than Ronaldo

Only Donald f’ing Trump would accept a made-up, bogus f’ing Peace Prize from FIFA and then effectively BAN 15 of the competing nations from attending the tournament.

Even if the Moron in Chief graciously allows some or all of the teams to attend the event; does that extend to their coaching staff, administrators, family members and possibly even to any fans who are prepared to risk the wrath of Trump’s mindless ICE warriors and travel there to show their support by cheering on their team, and by extension: their nation. 

Will they be attacked and sent to concentration camps if they cheer too loudly or (lord save us) if their team actually wins some games?

I have heard of nations boycotting international sports events in the past:@ mainly because of political instability or  social conscience but this is the first instance I know of that competitors are prevented from attending by the host nation!

Even if Trump rescinds his moronic edict you’d need more balls than I have got to risk their wellbeing and that of their friends and family to attend this tournament.

FIFA should either cancel the games in the USA or just give the f’ing cup to Trump before he nicks it. If the USA don’t win he’ll say it was stolen from them and the games were all “rigged”!

This is on course to being the worst World Cup Tournament in its history. 

FIFA are a totally corrupt organisation but even they must be getting green around the gills at the potential Cluster F**k that is impending when this competition (probably the last) starts to rewrite the history of the Beautiful Game in a manner that will not be soon forgotten.

text:
FIFA Time for FIFA to Revive an Elite Tournament of Continental Champions

It is time for FIFA to reconsider the organization of the FIFA Confederations Cup (or a FIFA Super Cup) as an official tournament among continental champions—without abolishing the FINALISSIMA, which has now established its own identity and appeal.

A tournament featuring confederation champions is far more meaningful, competitive, and historically valuable than events such as the FIFA Series or ordinary friendly matches. For the global audience, sponsors, and member associations, official competitions always carry far greater commercial value, prestige, and legitimacy.

⏳ Option 1: Held Before the FIFA World Cup 2026

The FIFA Confederations Cup / FIFA Super Cup could be staged in late March 2026, as an elite warm-up event ahead of:
FINALISSIMA 2026 (Argentina vs Spain), or
The FIFA World Cup 2026 (May–June)

Ideal participants:
1. FIFA World Cup 2022 Champions – Argentina
2. Copa América 2024 Champions – Argentina
(represented by Colombia as runners-up to maintain competitive balance)
3. UEFA EURO 2024 Champions – Spain
4. AFCON 2025 Champions – Senegal
5. CONCACAF Gold Cup 2025 Champions – Mexico
6. AFC Asian Cup Champions – Qatar
7. OFC Nations Cup 2024 Champions – New Zealand
8. Host nation

This format would deliver authentic intercontinental matchups, strong historical value, and enormous potential to attract global sponsors.

⏳ Option 2: Held After the FIFA World Cup

If a pre–World Cup schedule proves impractical, the FIFA Confederations Cup / FIFA Super Cup could be held in late December 2026 or throughout 2027, featuring:
1. FIFA World Cup 2026 Champions

2. Copa América 2024 Champions – Argentina
3. UEFA EURO 2024 Champions – Spain
4. AFCON 2026 Champions – Senegal
5. CONCACAF Gold Cup 2025 Champions – Mexico
6. AFC Asian Cup 2023 Champions – Qatar
7. OFC Nations Cup 2024 Champions – New Zealand
8. Host nation

This tournament would serve as a prestigious conclusion to the international competition cycle while preserving continuity among elite champions.

👉 Reform of Other FIFA Tournaments

1. FIFA Club World Cup
The tournament should be changed from a four-year cycle to a two-year cycle.
A four-year interval is too long and no longer aligned with the dynamics of modern football.

2. FIFA U-15 World Cup
FIFA should establish a FIFA U-15 World Cup, following CONMEBOL’s example with the Copa América U-15.
This would strengthen global grassroots development and help equalize talent pathways worldwide.

3. Integration of Olympic Football
Starting with the 2028 Olympics, FIFA should fully collaborate with the Olympic organizers so that:
Olympic football is officially designated as the FIFA U-23 World Cup .

🏆The champions receive an official FIFA trophy
🏆Official individual awards are presented:
🥇Golden Ball
🥇Golden Boot
🥇Golden Glove

This step would end the long-standing ambiguity surrounding the status of Olympic football and elevate it to the same level as other official FIFA tournaments .

text:
Pre-Show & Opening Ceremony.

[00:09] – Pre-show scenes and crowd atmosphere.

[06:04] – Announcement that the final draw will begin in 20 minutes.

[24:58] – Opening Performance: Legendary maestro Andrea Bocelli performs to kick off the show.

[29:48] – Hosts Introduction: Heidi Klum and Kevin Hart welcome the audience and introduce the event from the John F. Kennedy Center.

[31:38] – Red Carpet Interviews: Danny Ramirez speaks with Eli Manning and Wyclef Jean (representing Haiti).

Official Speeches & Awards.

[35:47] – FIFA President's Address: Gianni Infantino gives his opening speech, welcoming the host nations and dignitaries.

[37:09] – Dignitary Welcome: Introduction of US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump, followed by Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney (as identified in the video) and Mexican representatives.
[46:50] – Musical Performance: Robbie Williams and Nicole Scherzinger perform the new anthem "Driven by Desire."

[51:35] – FIFA Peace Prize: Infantino announces the inaugural FIFA Peace Prize.

[55:10] – Presentation: The Peace Prize is awarded to President Donald Trump for his diplomatic efforts.

[59:22] – Acceptance Speech: President Trump gives his acceptance speech.

Interviews & Host Nation Segment.

[01:03:13] – Interview: Lionel Scaloni (Argentina’s World Cup-winning coach) is interviewed.

[01:04:39] – Interview: Danny Ramirez interviews Brazilian legend Roberto Carlos.

[01:09:40] – Host Nation Allocation: Infantino returns to "change the draw" procedure. He invites Mark Carney and Donald Trump to stage to ceremonially draw their own countries (Canada and USA) into their groups.

[01:21:17] – Interview: Chat with Mexican legend Hugo Sánchez.
Halftime Performance & Preparation

[01:25:41] – Musical Performance: Ms. Lauryn Hill performs, joined by her son YG Marley.

[01:31:46] – Interview: Conversation with US women's soccer legend Carli Lloyd.

[01:47:53] – Pot 1 Draw: Conducted by Tom Brady.
Draws top seeds including Brazil, Germany, Netherlands, Belgium, Spain, Argentina, France, Portugal, and England.

[01:57:48] – Pot 2 Draw: Conducted by Shaquille O'Neal.

Draws South Africa, Qatar, Paraguay, Côte d'Ivoire, Tunisia, Egypt, Scotland, Saudi Arabia, Algeria, Uzbekistan, Panama, and Norway.

[02:19:36] – Pot 4 Draw: Conducted by Wayne Gretzky.
Draws the playoff winners and remaining teams: European Playoff winners, Haiti, Cabo Verde, Jordan, Ghana, and New Zealand.

Closing.

[02:30:58] – Final Interview: Short interview with Ghana's Asamoah Gyan regarding their group with England.

[02:31:41] – Recap: A full recap of the 12 groups (A through L).

[02:33:40] – Outro: Hosts Kevin Hart and Heidi Klum close the show with the guests on stage.

text:
If anyone is wondering why Uzbekistan was skipped here is the reason:

The rules state that no two teams from the same confederation except for UEFA can be drawn into the same group, and that no more than two teams from UEFA can be in the same group. The reason Uzbekistan couldn't be placed in group I has to do with the intercontinental play off paths in pot 4. Specifically, IC path 2 has Bolivia from Conmebol, Suriname from Concacaf and Iraq from AFC. By this point in the draw, the only 2 groups available for the winner of this path were groups I and L. Every other group already had one team from at least one of the 3 aforementioned confederations. If Uzbekistan were to be placed in group I, this would add a team from AFC to the group. Since group L already had two teams from UEFA, this would force Norway into group K and Panama into group L. However, this would mean that a Concacaf team would've been added to group L, leaving no available groups for the IC path 2 winner, resulting in an invalid draw. Therefore Uzbekistan couldn't have been placed in group I. In fact, by this point in the draw, Norway was already guaranteed to be in group I. A similar situation would have occurred if Panama was drawn, where Panama would have to be placed in group K because putting them in group I would leave no available groups once again. In the actual draw, Norway was placed in group I and Panama was placed in group L, eliminating group L as a possible group for the IC path 2 winner, leaving group I as the only available group, which was indeed where the IC path 2 winner was placed in the end.

text: ■I would like to personally suggest to FIFA, and I would like the height and horizontal length of the goal net to be the same as before, and the vertical length of the goal net to be longer than before.■As much as possible, I don't want the ball to come out when the ball goes into the goal net.■When the goal is scored, it will be visually realistic to have the ball in the net space rather than the ball bouncing off the net.■I think if the ball goes deeper and swings the net a lot, the players and spectators will feel more exhilarated.■I think it can also have the effect of increasing players' motivation to score a goal.■In other words, if the players look at the wider net space, they will have the urge to score more goals.■The goalkeeper will also try to defend the ball more aggressively if he looks at the wider net space.■In addition, long verticals look better from afar than short verticals.■The holes of the goal net also look better in the square than in the regular hexagon.■Among the World Cups so far, the 1994 U.S. World Cup had the longest vertical length of the goal net.■The same length as or up to 1.5 times the vertical length of the goal net of the 1994 U.S. World Cup would be sufficient.■It's not difficult at all, and it just need to prepare more net, so I think it's well worth reviewing.

text:
On February 11, 2025, Real Madrid staged a dramatic late comeback to defeat Manchester City 3–2 at the Etihad Stadium in the first leg of their UEFA Champions League knockout playoff tie.  Despite leading twice through Erling Haaland's goals, City conceded two goals in the final minutes, leaving them with a challenging task for the return leg at the Santiago Bernabéu. 


---

⚽ Match Summary

19' – Erling Haaland (Manchester City): Opened the scoring with a powerful finish, giving City an early lead. 

60' – Kylian Mbappé (Real Madrid): Equalized with a low shot that beat Ederson, bringing Madrid back into the game. 

80' – Erling Haaland (Manchester City): Restored City's lead by converting a penalty after a foul in the box.

86' – Brahim Díaz (Real Madrid): Capitalized on a defensive error to level the score, setting the stage for a dramatic finish.

90+2' – Jude Bellingham (Real Madrid): Scored the winning goal in stoppage time, completing Madrid's comeback and silencing the Etihad crowd. 



---

🔍 Key Takeaways

City's Defensive Vulnerabilities: Manchester City's defense faltered in the closing stages, conceding two late goals that turned a potential victory into a defeat.  

Madrid's Resilience: Real Madrid demonstrated their trademark resilience and ability to perform under pressure, turning the game around in the final minutes.  

Haaland's Brilliance: Erling Haaland's brace showcased his clinical finishing, but it wasn't enough to secure a win for City.  

Bellingham's Impact: Jude Bellingham's stoppage-time winner highlighted his growing influence in crucial moments for Real Madrid.  



---

📺 Match Highlights

For a visual recap of the match, you can watch the highlights here:


---

This result leaves Manchester City needing a win in the second leg at the Bernabéu to progress in the competition.  Real Madrid, buoyed by their late heroics, will look to capitalize on their advantage and secure a place in the next round. 

---

text:
ممثل الولاية: إنهاء الفظائع
قال ممثل ولاية ديلاوير وزعيم الأقلية في مجلس النواب مايكل رامون إنه منزعج من القسوة التي تم تصويرها في الفيلم الوثائقي. وقال: "كل يوم، يقع المزيد والمزيد من الناس ضحايا، وهذا مؤلم. إنه يؤلمك من الداخل، خاصة بعد رؤية شخص كان ضحية بالفعل، ولكن لحسن الحظ نجا".

أعرب ممثل ولاية ديلاوير مايكل رامون (يمين) عن دعمه لتشنغ بيمينج وممارسي الفالون دافا المضطهدين الآخرين.

قال النائب رامون، وهو أيضًا مرشح لمنصب حاكم الولاية، إنه سيواصل الجهود لإنهاء جريمة حصاد الأعضاء. وأوضح: "عندما نسأل الناس، لماذا يتم مواجهة [الجهود لإنهاء حصاد الأعضاء القسري] بالمقاومة؟ يشعر البعض بالقلق بشأن العواقب السياسية. هذا لا يتعلق بالسياسة، بل يتعلق بحياة الناس". بدأ النائب رامون القرار المشترك رقم 143 (HR143) لمجلس النواب الذي يدين حصاد الأعضاء القسري في الصين. وقد تم تمرير القرار في مجلس نواب ولاية ديلاوير في يونيو/حزيران.

عضو مجلس الشيوخ يدعو إلى مزيد من العمل

عضو مجلس الشيوخ ديفيد لوسون

شارك عضو مجلس الشيوخ ديفيد لوسون في رعاية HCR143 واقترح قرارًا مماثلًا في عام 2022، لكن قيادة مجلس الشيوخ عرقلته. وقد تأثر بشدة بالفيلم الوثائقي، وقال: "إنه لأمر مفجع عندما تختفي عائلتك ولا توجد نهاية. لا يمكنك الحصول على إغلاق.

"فيلم مظلم للغاية، وهو شيء نحتاج إلى الاستيقاظ عليه قبل أن يكون لدينا المزيد من الإبادة الجماعية، والمزيد من الموت، والمزيد من جرائم القتل في الصين"، وتابع، مضيفًا أن قصة السيد تشنغ "مقنعة للغاية، وكيف خرج هو معجزة. ... إنه الصوت في الظلام"، كما قال.

كما دعا السناتور لوسون إلى اتخاذ إجراءات فورية. وقال: "أولاً، توقفوا عن دعم الصين [الشيوعية]... يمكننا التوقف عن دعم الصين على الجانب الاقتصادي". يعتقد أن إدخال قوانين لمنع أولئك الذين يشاركون في زراعة الأعضاء في الصين من الحصول على تغطية تأمينية هي فكرة رائعة للمساعدة في إنهاء هذه الجريمة. وأوضح قائلاً: "الشيء الكبير الذي سمعته هنا الليلة هو منع التأمين من دفع ثمنها. أعتقد أن هذا سيكون خطوة كبيرة، وهذا ما يمكننا القيام به".

لقد أقرت عدة ولايات، بما في ذلك أيداهو ويوتا وتكساس، مشاريع قوانين تنص على أن شركات التأمين لا يمكنها تعويض الأميركيين عن إجراءات زراعة الأعضاء التي تتم في الصين. كما أن عدداً من الدول، بما في ذلك إسرائيل وإسبانيا وإيطاليا وتايوان والنرويج وبلجيكا وكندا وكوريا الجنوبية، لديها قوانين تمنع التأمين الطبي من دفع تكاليف جراحات زراعة الأعضاء في الصين.
